title: carlina corymbosa var. graeca
intro: Carlina corymbosa var. graeca, also known as Greek hawk's beard, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation. Carlina corymbosa var. graeca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
